commands.repair_reason: - [P1] This needs contributor proof and maintainer sequencing with the open stacked transport PR; automation should not repair around a missing real-behavior proof gate.; Needs attention: The stacked server diff touches a realtime authz boundary and currently leaves the dedicated progress scope behind a broader messages:write gate.; Review finding: [P2] Move progress off the messages:write gate — apps/api/internal/h... (sha=e9b513f442f3ebd65cb7ece248dcca9f5da92299)
commands.review_summary:
This draft PR stacks the server `agent.progress` primitive and adds a Svelte web consumer, generated embedded web assets, bot-scope docs, and server tests.

Reproducibility: yes. By source inspection, the PR adds a dedicated `agent_progress:write` branch while the handler still requires `messages:write` before it parses `body.Type`, and the new progress interval lacks the existing destroy-path cleanup pattern.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This needs contributor proof and maintainer sequencing with the open stacked transport PR; automation should not repair around a missing real-behavior proof gate.

target.title: feat(web): render agent.progress as ephemeral activity lines
target.body:
## Consumer half of the `agent.progress` producer/consumer contract

This is the **web client consumer** for the `agent.progress` ephemeral event. The **producer + transport + authorization** primitive is #23; this PR renders what that primitive delivers. It adds no server behavior.

### ⚠️ Stacked on #23 — review the web layer
```
main (incl. iOS Safari fix #21)
 └─ agent.progress primitive  (#23, producer/transport/authz — 5 server files)
     └─ render agent.progress as ephemeral activity lines  (THIS PR — web consumer)
```
Until #23 merges, this diff **also shows #23's 5 server files**. Once #23 lands, this reduces to the **13-file web-consumer delta**. The files to review here are `apps/web/src/ChatApp.svelte` and the new `apps/web/src/components/messages/AgentProgress.svelte` (plus the regenerated embedded `dist/` bundle).

### What it does
- **`AgentProgress.svelte`** (new): per-turn line list with kind icons (`tool ⚙`, `thinking/commentary ✦`, `plan ☰`, `patch ±`, `command_output ›`, `error ✕`); finalized lines dim to 0.55; strip caps at ~132px and ellipsizes. Rendered in an accented strip between the message list and composer, mirroring the existing `TypingIndicator` slot.
- **`ChatApp.svelte`** (+68): consumes the `agent.progress` realtime event — `op` append/update/finalize/clear keyed by `turn_id` + `line.id`, scoped to the active channel/DM view only, cleared on view switch, 45s TTL sweeper mirroring the typing-indicator pattern.
- **Ephemeral by construction**: nothing is persisted; reconnect/reload starts clean, matching the event's transport semantics in #23.

### Verification
| Check | Result |
|---|---|
| `cd apps/web && pnpm typecheck` | exit 0 |
| `pnpm build:web && pnpm embed:webassets` | dist regenerated from merged source; `agent-progress` CSS + handler present in bundle |
| `go build ./apps/api/internal/webassets/ ./apps/api/internal/httpapi/` | OK |
| `go test ./apps/api/internal/httpapi/ -run AgentProgress -count=1` | PASS (5.3s) |

The `ChatApp.svelte` edits and #21's iOS edits touch disjoint regions, so the rebase produced no source conflict; only the generated `webassets/dist/*` differed and was rebuilt (not hand-merged).

### Visual
The render is a gold-accented strip that lists the live turn's progress lines and dims them as they finalize before the TTL clears them — a non-persistent activity surface, distinct from durable message history. A faithful component-level render (real CSS/DOM, mock turn data) across the idle → live → finalized lifecycle is included under `.proof/agent-progress-render/` (reproducible via the bundled `montage.html`). A full running-app capture (dev-bootstrap server + a bot token with `agent_progress:write` posting real frames) can be added on request.
target.author: ragesaq
